items.title: Post-attendance earnings context by school and field of study
items.claim: College Scorecard reports aggregate post-attendance earnings context for students grouped by institution and field of study, which can frame the typical earnings range a program tends to be associated with. These figures describe cohorts of past students, so they may indicate direction rather than what any one person could earn.
items.limitations: Earnings are program- or school-level aggregates drawn from past cohorts of federally aided students with a reporting lag, so selection, timing, and coverage differences mean they are not an individual earnings prediction.

items.title: Completion and graduation context by institution
items.claim: College Scorecard summarizes school-level completion and graduation patterns, which can help frame how often students who enroll at a given institution tend to finish within tracked windows. These are institution-wide aggregates, so they may signal the general pattern rather than one student's likelihood of completing.
items.limitations: Completion figures are school-level aggregates over tracked time windows and student populations, so differences in student mix, program, and enrollment intensity mean they are not an individual completion prediction.

items.title: Cost, net price, and admissions context by institution
items.claim: College Scorecard reports school-level cost, average net price, and admissions context that can frame the typical financial commitment and selectivity associated with attending a given institution. These are institution-wide aggregates, so actual net price may vary by family circumstances and aid.
items.limitations: Cost and admissions figures are school-level averages that depend on a student's aid eligibility and the reporting year, so they describe typical context rather than the price or admissions outcome for any individual.
